name: Mexican Corn Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the corn from the cobs and scrape them thoroughly to extract the "milk."
2. Combine the corn, extracted "milk," and water in a blender. Blend briefly to break up kernels without over-blending.
3. Press the mixture through a fine sieve to extract as much liquid as possible. Place the strained solids in a saucepan.
4. Add butter to the solids and simmer gently for five minutes, stirring well to prevent sticking.
5. Incorporate milk and salt to taste. Bring the mixture to a boil and add chopped green chilies.
6. When ready to serve, stir in melted jack cheese until incorporated. Serve hot in bowls, garnished with deep-fried tortilla squares.
NOTE: Cut six or eight tortillas into cubes about half an inch square. Fry the cubes in hot fat, stirring occasionally, until crisp and golden.
